text:
This might be the most powerful AI and data awareness video yet. The Pause Before You Post campaign from @dpcrireland shows how fast tech is moving compared to child protection. Parents now share around 63 photos of their kids every month, often without realizing how far those images can travel.

Only 20 photos are enough for AI to build a convincing deepfake. Even worse, half of the child images found on criminal forums come from public posts made by parents themselves.

The campaign shows how simple details like a child’s name, birthday, school uniform, daily routine or friend group can turn into data points for AI systems. Piece by piece, strangers can rebuild a child’s world from images that were meant to be harmless.

In a digital world where faces, habits and locations can be tracked in seconds, every upload becomes permanent data that can be copied or misused.

What do you think parents should do to keep their kids safe online?

👉👉Follow @aiupdates.hub for more fascinating AI and robotics breakthroughs 
🎥: An Coimisiún um Chosaint Sonraí Data Protection Commission

#technology #ai #innovation #digitalsafety #parentingtips #artificialintelligence #ainews #technews #technology #automation
